response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software developer, but the profile does not match this role's core requirements. C++ experience is limited to a short stint at National Instruments (~6 months, over a decade ago) and a thesis project, far from the required 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. There is no SQL Server/Oracle depth, no Fixed Income or Capital Markets domain experience (RIDE Capital was retail investment tooling in Node/TypeScript, not capital-markets systems), and no Summit exposure. Strong general engineering fundamentals do not offset the missing mandatory skills.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured engineering career with strong testing/TDD and CI/CD practices
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang microservices handling millions of requests/sec)
Solid communication skills evidenced by workshops, talks, and teaching experience
concerns: Minimal and outdated C++ experience; nowhere near the required 5–10 years on Windows
No Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain knowledge
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; background is MongoDB/MySQL and web stacks

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain expertise
Strong general engineering and communication fundamentals
